metadata.summary: Name every agent tool as verb_object and prefix with service namespace to improve tool selection accuracy through lexical clarity and collision avoidance in multi-service catalogs.

content.title: Verb-Object Tool Naming with Namespace Prefix
content.summary: Name every agent tool as verb_object (create_issue, search_files) and prefix with a service namespace (github_create_issue, asana_search_tasks) when the catalog spans multiple servers or MCP gateways. Tool names matter MORE than descriptions for selection accuracy — the model parses the name first and uses the description only to disambiguate close matches. Single-token, opaque names (search, manage, do_thing) are the most common reason an agent picks the wrong tool.

why.p: Anthropic's tool-writing guidance reports that prefix and naming schemes meaningfully change selection accuracy on internal evals, and that small renames produce outsized swings. The mechanism: the model treats the tool list as part of the prompt, and selection is biased by lexical similarity between the user's request and the tool name. A name like search collides with every other server's search in an aggregator; a name like manage_files forces the model to read the full description on every turn just to figure out what verbs the tool actually supports. verb_object mirrors how engineers name CRUD endpoints, which Claude has seen millions of times in pre-training.

list.item: Any tool catalog with five or more tools.
Anytime tools come from multiple MCP servers or pass through an MCP gateway/aggregator.
When wrapping third-party SDKs that already use verb_object (GitHub, Stripe, Asana, Jira) — keep the verb, add the namespace.
During tool-eval iteration: rename, re-run eval, keep the better name.
Single-tool hobby scripts where there is only ever one search and no aggregator — naming is not the bottleneck.
Inside an SDK that already enforces a non-snake_case convention (e.g., GraphQL camelCase) — match the host convention instead of fighting it.
Do not pad with marketing nouns (acme_corp_internal_v2_search_documents_advanced) — long names hurt context budget and selection alike.

section.name: naming-rule
section.p: Tool names are part of the prompt. The model selects by name first, description second. A vague or ambiguous name forces the model to read every description on every turn, which collapses selection accuracy as the catalog grows.

rule.statement: Every tool name MUST be lowercase snake_case in the form verb_object (e.g., create_issue, search_files). When the agent sees tools from more than one service or MCP server, prepend a service namespace (github_create_issue, stripe_refund_charge).
rule.rationale: Anthropic's internal evals show prefix and naming schemes meaningfully change selection accuracy; verb_object mirrors CRUD endpoint conventions Claude saw millions of times in pre-training, while namespaces eliminate name collisions inside MCP aggregators.

example.title: GitHub and Asana tools with namespaces
example.note: Verb is unambiguous, object is explicit, namespace eliminates collision with asana_* and any future gitlab_*.

example.title: Vague names without namespace
example.note: search collides with every other server's search in a gateway. manage hides three verbs behind one name — the model must guess. do_thing carries zero signal.

antipattern.description: Polymorphic verb tools that take an action enum (manage_files(action="copy"|"move"|"delete", ...)).
antipattern.reason: The model has to read the description and remember which kwargs are valid per action. Atomic verbs (copy_file, move_file, delete_file) are self-describing and have typed schemas the runtime can validate.
Long names burn context budget, dilute the verb, and hurt lexical match with the user's request.

template.purpose: One-line checks before shipping a tool name.
